name: conventions
description: Load coding conventions and style guidelines when writing or reviewing code. Ensures consistency with project standards.
capabilities: Loads project coding conventions from conventions.json
Provides naming convention guidelines for code elements
Supplies file organization and structure standards
Defines error handling and logging patterns
author: Clouder0
securitySummary: This is a prompt-based knowledge skill with no executable code. It only defines metadata for loading coding conventions from conventions.json. The static scanner produced false positives by misidentifying metadata fields (URLs, hashes, skill name) as security threats. No network access, no file system operations beyond reading convention files, and no external commands.
valueStatement: Teams need consistent coding standards to maintain code quality across projects. This skill loads project conventions to ensure code follows established patterns during writing and review.
limitations: Does not enforce conventions automatically on code
Requires manual activation by the user

useCases.title: Write Standards-Compliant Code
useCases.description: Write new code that follows project conventions and team standards

useCases.title: Review Code Compliance
useCases.description: Review pull requests for convention compliance and style adherence

useCases.title: Define Team Standards
useCases.description: Ensure team codebases maintain consistent patterns and conventions

promptTemplates.title: Load Project Conventions
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting work on a new project or codebase

promptTemplates.title: Check Convention Compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code for convention compliance

promptTemplates.title: Ask Convention Questions
promptTemplates.scenario: Uncertain about naming or style requirements
promptTemplates.prompt: What does our convention say about naming async functions?

promptTemplates.title: Generate with Standards
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating new files following project patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a new service file following our conventions

bestPractices: Load conventions at the start of each coding session
Update conventions.json when project standards evolve
antiPatterns: Writing code without checking conventions first
Ignoring established naming patterns in the codebase
Skipping convention checks during code reviews

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude Code and Claude. Check tool documentation for version compatibility.

faq.question: What convention areas are supported?
faq.answer: Naming, file organization, error handling, testing, and documentation conventions.

faq.question: How do I add conventions to my project?
faq.answer: Create conventions.json in memory/knowledge/codebase/ with your project standards.

faq.question: Is my convention data secure?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads local files. No data is transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why are my conventions not loading?
faq.answer: Verify conventions.json exists at the correct path and contains valid JSON.

faq.question: How is this different from linters?
faq.answer: Conventions provide guidelines while linters automatically enforce syntax rules.
